title: Package manifest — bundle of related analyses / layers / panels / pages
description: Per ADAPTER_CONTRACT.md §7. A package is the unit of distribution: one folder under packages/<package_id>/ with a manifest.json listing the analyses, layers, panels, and pages that go together. Packages are how the user picks a body of work ("discovery+karyotype", "relatedness chain", "popgen scans") without naming each piece individually.

atlas_id.description: Owning atlas (e.g. inversion_atlas). Optional for cross-atlas packages.

analyses.description: Analyses bundled by this package. Each is the analysis_id, which must resolve to analysis_registry.

required.description: If false, the analysis is optional within the package (useful for stubs).

rationale.description: Why this analysis belongs in the package.

layers.description: Layers the package contributes (produced by its analyses) and / or consumes from outside.

role.description: produced = an analysis in this package produces it; consumed_internal = panel inside this package renders it; consumed_external = panel in another package renders it.

panels.description: Panel IDs this package owns. Each panel_id must resolve to a panel_v1 entry.

pages.description: Optional. Pages whose hooks this package depends on. The page is not 'owned' by a package — multiple packages can contribute panels to the same page.

requires_packages.description: Other package_ids whose analyses / layers this one depends on. Informational dependency hint.
